unmapped_risk: A US Treasury/Commerce joint directive classifying NVDA's current-generation data center GPUs as 'critical infrastructure components' under new executive orders, triggering immediate mandatory audit and seizure of all chips in transit or held by non-US sovereign buyers on national security grounds. This mechanism freezes $15B+ in committed revenue overnight with no legal recourse for buyers, creating a sudden-death regulatory cliff that markets treat as a supply chain risk rather than a demand-side collapse.
underweighted_risk: The 'GPU-to-Power Ratio' regulatory feedback loop where regional grid interconnection moratoriums halt new data center permits until transformer manufacturing capacity catches up. This is more dangerous than consensus believes because regulators are shifting from permitting reviews to hard physical caps on power density, instantly invalidating multi-year CapEx deployment pipelines. Markets underweight this by modeling grid expansion as linear infrastructure growth, ignoring that high-voltage switchgear and transformer supply chains face 24-month lead times and raw material bottlenecks that cannot be accelerated by capital alone.
structural_fragility: NVDA's revenue concentration on a single advanced packaging node (TSMC CoWoS) combined with a hyper-concentrated options gamma profile creates a double-exposure to non-linear shocks: any production halt or regulatory freeze instantly translates to both supply-side collapse and demand-side deleveraging, with no operational hedge.
contagion_path: NVDA revenue freeze → TSM loses primary CoWoS utilization catalyst → AVGO networking ASICs lose cluster deployment pipeline → LITE optical interconnect orders delayed across hyperscaler deployments → SMH suffers sector-wide de-rating as the 'AI demand anchor' thesis breaks simultaneously across multiple positions.
watch_items: US Commerce Department Entity List or Dual-Use Infrastructure classification updates for advanced computing chips
Regional grid interconnection queue wait times in major hyperscaler deployment zones (Texas, Northern Virginia, Singapore)
